title: Teardown-Driven Competitive Benchmarking
gist: A promotional TechInsights landing page arguing that teardown-driven benchmarking (die-level analysis, verified component IDs, BOM cost models) yields more reliable competitive intelligence than spec sheets or public cost estimates. Marketing copy, not analytical research.

insights.claim: Competitive analysis based on spec sheets, marketing claims, or high-level cost estimates systematically misses what is physically inside a product and how it was engineered.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Investors relying on vendor-published specs may be mispricing competitive moats; physical teardown data is a differentiated edge source.
insights.quote: Most competitive analysis stops at spec sheets, marketing claims, or high-level cost estimates. That misses the most critical insights

insights.claim: Die-level teardown analysis can reveal process node, IP, packaging, and second-source supplier choices that are not disclosed in public product documentation.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Detecting second-source suppliers and process-node shifts ahead of disclosure can flag supply-chain risk or margin changes for chip names.
